title: Users who don't have access to boards in a workspace should not see it in the browse all function
excerpt: When going to browse all workspaces, users can see workspace names even though they dont have access to them or the boards in that workspace. IMO they shouldn’t know that the workspace even exists.

posts.author: isaack (Isaac Klugmann)
posts.cookedHtml: When going to browse all workspaces, users can see workspace names even though they dont have access to them or the boards in that workspace. IMO they shouldn’t know that the workspace even exists.

posts.author: TeamTrivia (Frank Griffin)
posts.cookedHtml: I can’t believe that any user can browse all workspaces and then go to any board even if they are not a “Member” of that board. This is a dumb idea, unless you are trying to sell Enterprise plans and you don’t care about providing access controls for your other plans. If I would have know this before I bought Monday.com, I would have gone with one of the Monday.com competitors.
